skill.name: agent-memory-systems
skill.description: Guides the design of agent memory systems, including short-term context, long-term vector storage, chunking, retrieval, and memory governance patterns.
skill.summary: Design retrieval-focused memory systems for AI agents.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged duplicate Ruby or shell backtick execution patterns at SKILL.md:67 and a weak cryptography pattern at SKILL.md:3. Review found line 67 contains Markdown inline code for related skill names, and line 3 is plain metadata description text. No evidence found of command execution, weak cryptography, prompt injection, network access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The four duplicate external command alerts at SKILL.md:67 are false positives. The backticks format related skill names in Markdown and do not execute Ruby, shell, or any external command.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete line is a prose list of related skills inside Markdown inline code. There is no executable context, script block, or command invocation.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern
low_findings.description: The high-severity weak cryptography alert at SKILL.md:3 is a false positive. The line is YAML frontmatter containing a natural language description of agent memory systems and does not reference cryptographic algorithms.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed line contains descriptive metadata only. No MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or other weak cryptographic usage is present on that line.

content.value_statement: AI agents often fail when they store information but cannot retrieve the right memory. This skill helps teams design memory types, chunking, vector stores, and retrieval strategies.
actual_capabilities: Explains short-term, long-term, working, episodic, semantic, and procedural memory concepts.
Guides selection of memory types for different agent information needs.
Highlights vector store selection as a design pattern for agent memory.
Emphasizes chunking strategy as a key factor in retrieval quality.
Identifies common memory anti-patterns, including storing everything forever.
limitations: Provides conceptual guidance, not runnable code or a packaged memory service.
Does not include vendor-specific vector database setup instructions.
Does not benchmark embedding models or retrieval settings.
The source file is brief and some description text appears truncated.

use_cases.title: Plan Agent Memory Architecture
use_cases.description: Map short-term, long-term, and working memory roles before building an AI agent.

use_cases.title: Improve Retrieval Quality
use_cases.description: Review chunking, metadata filtering, and temporal scoring choices for an existing memory system.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Memory Design Risks
use_cases.description: Identify patterns that cause stale, conflicting, or irrelevant memories in agent workflows.

prompt_templates.title: Choose Memory Types
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose memory types for an AI agent that supports users across repeated sessions. Compare short-term, long-term, episodic, semantic, and procedural memory.

prompt_templates.title: Design Retrieval Strategy
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my agent memory plan and propose a retrieval strategy. Include chunking, metadata filters, recency, and conflict handling.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Existing Memory System
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze this agent memory design for likely failure modes. Focus on stale memories, poor chunking, missing metadata, and retrieval ranking problems.

prompt_templates.title: Create Memory Operating Rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft operating rules for forming, retrieving, updating, and expiring memories in a production AI agent. Include testing and monitoring criteria.

output_examples.input: Design memory for a customer support agent.
output_examples.output: A layered memory plan with session context, customer profile facts, prior issue history, and retrieval rules based on metadata and recency.

output_examples.input: Review why my agent remembers irrelevant details.
output_examples.output: A diagnosis focused on overbroad chunks, missing metadata filters, weak retrieval ranking, and lack of decay for older memories.

output_examples.input: Define memory anti-patterns for our engineering review.
output_examples.output: A concise review checklist covering permanent storage, untested chunking, single memory type usage, and missing conflict detection.

best_practices: Design retrieval rules before expanding storage volume.
Test chunk sizes with realistic user questions and expected memories.
Track metadata such as source, time, embedding model, and memory type.
anti_patterns: Store every interaction forever without retention rules.
Use one memory type for all facts, events, and procedures.
Chunk documents without measuring retrieval accuracy.

faq.question: What does this skill help me design?
faq.answer: It helps design memory architecture for AI agents, with emphasis on retrieval, chunking, and memory type selection.

faq.question: Does it provide executable code?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides design guidance and review prompts, not a runnable implementation.

faq.question: Which tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does it choose a vector database for me?
faq.answer: It frames vector store selection as a design pattern, but does not provide vendor-specific installation steps.

faq.question: Can it help debug memory failures?
faq.answer: Yes. It focuses on retrieval, chunking, metadata, temporal scoring, and conflict handling as common failure points.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found only false positive static findings and no evidence of malicious behavior.
